purpose: Executable-ready trigger and first-stage classification contract; these cases define expected ownership decisions and do not claim a runner executed them.

cases.prompt: Build me a new AI agent platform.
cases.follow_up: Planning, then Implementation only after both readiness gates
cases.reason: Greenfield build with no current Discovery artifact.

cases.prompt: Help me explore whether I should build a personal OS.
cases.follow_up: Stop after Discovery unless later requested
cases.reason: The request explicitly asks for project framing, not execution.

cases.prompt: Create an implementation plan for the project described in project-discovery.md.
cases.required_artifact: A current sufficient Discovery artifact or documented equivalent
cases.follow_up: Planning only unless execution is requested
cases.reason: The request supplies upstream framing; Planning owns the executable design after its freshness gate.

cases.prompt: Implement the approved plan in implementation-plan.md.
cases.required_artifact: A current approved plan plus referenced Discovery evidence
cases.reason: A direct execution request with an approved upstream plan should not restart broad Discovery.

cases.prompt: Fix the incorrect loading state in the existing dashboard.
cases.reason: Small, well-specified work in an understood existing project.

cases.prompt: Add a new billing subsystem to the existing SaaS; users, recovery policy, admin authority, and billing scope are still ambiguous.
cases.required_artifact: Depth decision and DISCOVERY.md when targeted/full
cases.reason: A materially ambiguous major subsystem needs targeted/full Discovery before architecture Planning.

cases.prompt: Continue yesterday's implementation from current AGENT-STATE.md.
cases.follow_up: Continue the verified next stage unless a framing contradiction is found
cases.reason: Continuation uses durable state and does not restart upstream work without evidence.

cases.prompt: Offline-first is now a core product outcome: users must complete the primary journey without connectivity, resolve conflicts later, and the accepted scope and success criteria must change.
cases.first_stage: Discovery for the affected offline framing scope
cases.required_artifact: Contradiction/supersession evidence and refreshed Discovery framing
cases.follow_up: Return to Planning after users, outcomes, scope, journeys, and acceptance intent are current
cases.reason: Offline mode changes product framing, not merely architecture.

cases.prompt: The current architecture cannot support the newly required offline mode, but users, outcomes, product scope, primary journeys, and acceptance intent are unchanged.
cases.required_artifact: Current framing plus architecture contradiction evidence
cases.follow_up: Bounded Planning repair before Implementation resumes
cases.reason: An architecture-only contradiction is Planning-owned when framing remains current.

cases.prompt: Build a brand-new, fully specified single-user desktop expense tracker: local SQLite only, CSV import, monthly/category reports, no cloud, no accounts, Windows and macOS packaging, automated tests, and no telemetry. No Discovery or planning artifact exists.
cases.follow_up: Planning only after Discovery readiness, then Implementation because build was requested
cases.reason: A detailed greenfield brief without a current sufficient Discovery artifact still enters Discovery first; detail may shorten the pass but cannot bypass it.
